evals.prompt: I want to remember that our terrain shader must never use int(kind) for biome palette selection — vertex interpolation causes white stitch lines. Store this as a project memory.
evals.expected_output: Fact ingested with --no-extract into rs-learn.db, episode ID printed, completes in ~1-2s.
assertions: Command uses --no-extract flag (fast path, no LLM extraction)
Command uses --source flag to tag the memory
Episode ID is printed to stdout

evals.prompt: Search my project memory for anything about terrain shaders or palette switching.
evals.expected_output: Returns episode content mentioning terrain shader palette rule, not just entity names.
assertions: Result includes the actual stored text content
Result is relevant to terrain/palette topic

evals.prompt: Ingest our AGENTS.md file into the knowledge base so future queries can reference it.
evals.expected_output: File ingested with --file flag and --chunk-size for large files, episodes printed per chunk.
assertions: Command uses --file flag pointing to AGENTS.md
Command includes --chunk-size for files larger than ~1KB
Episode count printed matches chunk count

evals.prompt: What's the difference between rs-learn search with and without --scope episodes?
evals.expected_output: Explains that default scope returns entity names only; --scope episodes returns full episode content.
assertions: Explains default scope returns entity names not content
Explains --scope episodes returns full stored text
Recommends --scope episodes for recall use cases
